skill.name: tailwind-css
skill.description: Guides Tailwind CSS styling for responsive layouts, utility-first components, custom themes, dark mode, and accessibility-focused UI work.
skill.summary: Build responsive Tailwind CSS interfaces with mobile-first utilities, themes, dark mode, and accessible component patterns.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium indicators, but the cited SKILL.md lines are Markdown code fences, Tailwind breakpoint syntax, JSX examples, and documentation references. I found no evidence of executable commands, data exfiltration, prompt injection, credential access, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to Markdown code fences, inline Tailwind class examples, and JSX template literals. These examples are instructional text inside SKILL.md and do not execute Ruby, shell, or system commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is static Markdown documentation and JSX examples, not code run by the skill. No shell command, Ruby backtick execution, or dynamic execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Tailwind Terms Flagged as High-Risk Blockers
low_findings.description: The high blocker detections align with words and tokens used for Tailwind CSS guidance, such as responsive prefixes, dark mode, and design documentation. I found no weak cryptographic algorithm usage or malware-like blocker behavior in the cited file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Tailwind configuration and UI requirements. There is no hashing, encryption, persistence, obfuscation, or control-flow behavior to support the static blocker labels.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Links Flagged as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are reference links to Tailwind CSS, Tailwind UI, shadcn/ui, and WCAG documentation. They do not instruct the agent to send data, download code, or contact an untrusted service during execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only under a References section and are documentation resources. No network API call, credential transfer, or exfiltration pattern is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: UI Layout Text Flagged as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections occur in responsive layout guidance and component examples. The skill does not ask to enumerate hostnames, environment variables, processes, users, filesystems, or network interfaces.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines discuss dashboard grids and responsive layout utilities. I found no semantic evidence of host or system reconnaissance intent.

content.value_statement: Responsive Tailwind interfaces can become inconsistent across components, themes, and breakpoints. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concrete rules for mobile-first layouts, utility classes, dark mode, and accessibility.
actual_capabilities: Applies mobile-first Tailwind breakpoints for responsive layouts.
Guides component styling with utility classes and variant patterns.
Suggests Tailwind theme extensions for colors, spacing, fonts, and dark mode.
Provides accessibility checks for contrast, focus states, and touch targets.
Covers common UI patterns such as KPI cards, dashboard grids, forms, and toggles.
Recommends performance practices for unused CSS and production builds.
limitations: It is focused on Tailwind CSS and does not cover plain CSS or CSS-in-JS workflows.
It provides guidance and examples, not a complete design system by itself.
It does not verify visual output unless paired with browser testing tools.
It assumes the project already uses or plans to use Tailwind CSS.

use_cases.title: Style a Product Dashboard
use_cases.description: Create responsive cards, grids, forms, and dark mode states for an operational dashboard.

use_cases.title: Standardize UI Components
use_cases.description: Convert repeated styling decisions into consistent Tailwind utilities, variants, and theme tokens.

use_cases.title: Improve Responsive Accessibility
use_cases.description: Review layouts for mobile fit, readable contrast, focus states, and touch-friendly controls.

prompt_templates.title: Style a Simple Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Style this component with Tailwind CSS. Use mobile-first classes, clear spacing, hover states, and accessible focus styles.

prompt_templates.title: Make a Layout Responsive
prompt_templates.prompt: Update this layout for mobile, tablet, and desktop. Use Tailwind breakpoints and explain the responsive decisions briefly.

prompt_templates.title: Add Theme and Dark Mode Support
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend the Tailwind theme for this interface. Add semantic colors, dark mode classes, and contrast-safe component states.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Tailwind UI Surface
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Tailwind UI for consistency, accessibility, mobile overflow, unused custom CSS, and production readiness.

output_examples.input: A dashboard card needs better mobile styling and dark mode support.
output: A mobile-first card layout with compact base spacing and larger spacing at tablet sizes.
Light and dark color choices with readable contrast.
Focus and loading states that match the rest of the dashboard.

output_examples.input: A settings form should work on phones and desktop screens.
output: A single-column mobile form that expands into a wider desktop layout.
Consistent labels, input states, and button sizing.
Touch targets and focus rings suitable for keyboard and mobile users.

output_examples.input: A project needs a Tailwind theme for reusable brand colors.
output: Semantic theme recommendations for primary, success, warning, and error colors.
Guidance for extending rather than replacing the default Tailwind theme.
Dark mode guidance that keeps tokens predictable across components.

best_practices: Start with unprefixed mobile styles, then add larger breakpoint variants only where needed.
Use semantic theme tokens for repeated colors, spacing, and typography decisions.
Validate contrast, focus states, and mobile touch targets before considering styling complete.
anti_patterns: Do not replace Tailwind with large custom CSS blocks for ordinary component styling.
Do not add desktop-only layouts before checking the 375px mobile viewport.
Do not use arbitrary values when an existing Tailwind scale value communicates the design clearly.

faq.question: Does this skill write Tailwind CSS code?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward Tailwind utility classes, component patterns, and configuration changes.

faq.question: Can it help with dark mode?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends class-based dark mode, dark variants, and contrast checks for both light and dark states.

faq.question: Does it support responsive design?
faq.answer: Yes. It emphasizes mobile-first styles and Tailwind breakpoints for tablet, desktop, and large screens.

faq.question: Is it useful with shadcn/ui?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill references shadcn/ui patterns and encourages consistent tokens and accessible component states.

faq.question: Will it test the rendered page automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives styling guidance, but visual verification requires browser testing or screenshots from the active project.

faq.question: Can it replace a designer?
faq.answer: No. It improves implementation quality, but product direction and visual identity still need human design judgment.
